structured_payload_candidate.title: Fault Response for Fans, Pumps, Cooling Towers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault: command next off equipment on while alarming equipment remains on; if that fails, repeat; set alarming equipment to last position; non-alarming equipment follow even wear logic.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Command next off equipment on; if it fails, repeat; move alarming equipment to last in staging order.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: i. For fans, pumps, and cooling towers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” remains commanded on. (b) If Equipment “B” fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), it shall remain commanded on and the preceding step shall be repeated until the quantity of equipment called for by the current stage has proven on, or there are no more available equipment. (c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position). (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic.

evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: i. For fans, pumps, and cooling towers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” remains commanded on. (b) If Equipment “B” fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), it shall remain commanded on and the preceding step shall be repeated until the quantity of equipment called for by the current stage has proven on, or there are no more available equipment. (c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position). (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fault Response for Chillers and Boilers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault: command next off equipment on while alarming equipment is commanded off and set to last position; if that fails, repeat; if all tried and insufficient non-alarming equipment, most recently alarmed remains on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Command next off equipment on; turn off alarming equipment; move to last position; repeat if needed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: ii. For chillers and boilers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” is commanded off and set to the last position in the lead/lag staging order. (b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on.

evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: ii. For chillers and boilers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” is commanded off and set to the last position in the lead/lag staging order. (b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Logic General Operation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Systems using Trim & Respond setpoint reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Trim & Respond logic resets the setpoint within SPmin to SPmax. When device is OFF, setpoint = SP0. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim setpoint by SPtrim; if R>I, respond by changing setpoint by SPres*(R-I) but no more than SPres-max.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Device proven ON, after delay Td
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Every time step T: if requests ≤ ignored, trim setpoint by SPtrim; if requests > ignored, increase setpoint by (R-I)*SPres but capped at SPres-max.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If there are more than I requests, respond by changing the setpoint by SPres*(R – I), (i.e., the number of requests minus the number of ignored requests) but no more than SPres-max.

evidence_quote: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.
anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
evidence_text: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Fault Condition - Manual Shut Off
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition exists if chiller is manually shut off as indicated by the status of the Local/Auto switch from chiller gateway.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Local/Auto switch indicates manual shut off
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger fault response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Chiller is manually shut off as indicated by the status of the Local/Auto switch from chiller gateway
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Chiller is manually shut off as indicated by the status of the Local/Auto switch from chiller gateway

structured_payload_candidate.title: Damper/Valve Position Determination Methods
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Acceptable methods for determining position: analog actuator (assume equal to analog signal), floating actuator with position feedback AI or position estimated by timing with autozeroing in Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use one of the acceptable methods to determine position.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following are acceptable methods for determining position: a. Analog actuator. Position may be assumed to be equal to analog signal to actuator. b. Floating actuator. Provide either 1. Position feedback AI ... 2. Position estimated by timing pulse-open and pulse-closed commands with autozeroing whenever zone is in Unoccupied Mode and damper is driven full closed.
